subtasks.description: Query Datadog license allocation data including tier, user count, instance count, and active service allocations from the SaaS entitlement tracking system
Retrieve Datadog actual usage metrics including DAU/MAU, instances monitored, logs ingested, and custom metrics usage with daily granularity over the past 12 months

Reconcile license allocations against actual usage metrics, calculate utilization rates, identify growth patterns, gap analysis, and generate trend classifications (overprovisioned/underutilized/optimized) with month-over-month growth rate analysis

plan.cost_rationale: This analysis requires three parallel data-collection tasks followed by sophisticated reconciliation. The sql_gen (mid tier) retrieves license allocation data from relational tables with standard joins. The mongo_query (mid tier) fetches time-series usage metrics and aggregations. The cross_recon task (frontier) performs complex reconciliation: aligning two data sources with different schemas, calculating multi-signal utilization ratios, time-series growth analysis with trend inference, and generating posture classifications—all requiring higher reasoning. The plan is minimal: it avoids unnecessary intermediate transforms and combines all analysis outputs into a single cross_recon subtask rather than splitting into separate reporting tasks.

output.sql: SELECT publisher, product, fiscal_year, fiscal_quarter, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) AS license_count, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS distinct_users, COUNT(event_id) AS total_events, AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, AVG(amount_usd) AS avg_amount_usd FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher ILIKE '%datadog%' GROUP BY publisher, product, fiscal_year, fiscal_quarter ORDER BY fiscal_year DESC, fiscal_quarter DESC, total_amount_usd DESC
